top_signals.title: Fed’s Barkin Says Repeated Supply Shocks Test Inflation Anchor
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin said the ability of businesses and consumers to tolerate the latest in a series of supply shocks will determine whether the US central bank can continue to “look through” higher inflation without raising interest rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Germany’s Rheinmetall Raises First Public Bond Since 2010
top_signals.summary: Debt investors appetite for Europe’s booming defense sector was on full display on Thursday as Rheinmetall AG drew more than €3.9 billion ($4.5 billion) of orders for its first conventional public bond sale since 2010.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
